{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_23a542a186cb4abeade1c0d2","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage server\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"fmt\"\n5\t\t\"net/http\"\n6\t\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n7\t\t\"testing\"\n8\t)\n9\t\n10\t// =============================================================================\n11\t// HTTP STATUS CODE VALIDATION HELPERS\n12\t// =============================================================================\n13\t// This module provides comprehensive HTTP status code validation utilities\n14\t// for error response testing. It supports single status codes, multiple allowed\n15\t// status codes, and flexible validation patterns.\n16\t//\n17\t// Functions:\n18\t// - ValidateStatusCode: Validate single expected status code\n19\t// - ValidateStatusCodeAny: Validate against multiple allowed status codes\n20\t// - ValidateStatusCodeRange: Validate status code is within range\n21\t// - CheckStatusCode: Non-asserting version that returns boolean\n22\t// - GetStatusCodeDescription: Get human-readable status code description\n23\t// =============================================================================\n24\t\n25\t// ValidateStatusCode validates that the HTTP response has the expected status code.\n26\t//\n27\t// This is the basic status code validation helper that checks if a response\n28\t// matches a single expected status code. It provides clear error messages\n29\t// indicating what was expected vs. what was received.\n30\t//\n31\t// Parameters:\n32\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n33\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n34\t//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n35\t//\n36\t// Example:\n37\t//   ValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)\n38\tfunc ValidateStatusCode(t *testing.T, response interface{}, expectedCode int) {\n39\t\tt.Helper()\n40\t\n41\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n42\t\tif actualCode != expectedCode {\n43\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code %d (%s), got %d (%s)\",\n44\t\t\t\texpectedCode, GetStatusCodeDescription(expectedCode),\n45\t\t\t\tactualCode, GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode))\n46\t\t}\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t// ValidateStatusCodeAny validates that the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n50\t//\n51\t// This helper is useful when multiple status codes are acceptable for a given scenario.\n52\t// For example, when testing authentication, you might accept both 403 (Forbidden)\n53\t// and 401 (Unauthorized) depending on the authentication method.\n54\t//\n55\t// Parameters:\n56\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n57\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n58\t//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n59\t//\n60\t// Example:\n61\t//   ValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{200, 201, 204})\n62\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeAny(t *testing.T, response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) {\n63\t\tt.Helper()\n64\t\n65\t\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n66\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"ValidateStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\")\n67\t\t\treturn\n68\t\t}\n69\t\n70\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n71\t\n72\t\t// Check if actual code is in the allowed list\n73\t\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n74\t\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n75\t\t\t\treturn // Success - code is allowed\n76\t\t\t}\n77\t\t}\n78\t\n79\t\t// Build helpful error message showing what was allowed vs. what was received\n80\t\tallowedDesc := make([]string, len(allowedCodes))\n81\t\tfor i, code := range allowedCodes {\n82\t\t\tallowedDesc[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, GetStatusCodeDescription(code))\n83\t\t}\n84\t\n85\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code to be one of [%v], got %d (%s)\",\n86\t\t\tallowedDesc, actualCode, GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode))\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\t// ValidateStatusCodeRange validates that the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n90\t//\n91\t// This helper is useful for validating status codes by category, such as:\n92\t// - 2xx for success responses (200-299)\n93\t// - 4xx for client errors (400-499)\n94\t// - 5xx for server errors (500-599)\n95\t//\n96\t// Parameters:\n97\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n98\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n99\t//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n100\t//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n101\t//\n102\t// Example:\n103\t//   // Validate 2xx success response\n104\t//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 200, 299)\n105\t//\n106\t//   // Validate 4xx client error\n107\t//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 400, 499)\n108\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRange(t *testing.T, response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) {\n109\t\tt.Helper()\n110\t\n111\t\tif minCode > maxCode {\n112\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"ValidateStatusCodeRange: minCode (%d) cannot be greater than maxCode (%d)\", minCode, maxCode)\n113\t\t}\n114\t\n115\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n116\t\n117\t\tif actualCode < minCode || actualCode > maxCode {\n118\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code in range [%d-%d] (%s), got %d (%s)\",\n119\t\t\t\tminCode, maxCode, getRangeDescription(minCode, maxCode),\n120\t\t\t\tactualCode, GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode))\n121\t\t}\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\t// CheckStatusCode checks if the HTTP response has the expected status code without asserting.\n125\t//\n126\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean, allowing for conditional logic\n127\t// in tests. Useful when you need to handle different status codes programmatically\n128\t// rather than failing the test immediately.\n129\t//\n130\t// Parameters:\n131\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n132\t//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n133\t//\n134\t// Returns:\n135\t//   - true if the response has the expected status code, false otherwise\n136\t//\n137\t// Example:\n138\t//   if CheckStatusCode(w, 404) {\n139\t//       // Handle 404 case\n140\t//   } else {\n141\t//       // Handle other cases\n142\t//   }\n143\tfunc CheckStatusCode(response interface{}, expectedCode int) bool {\n144\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n145\t\treturn actualCode == expectedCode\n146\t}\n147\t\n148\t// CheckStatusCodeAny checks if the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n149\t//\n150\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for flexible status code checking.\n151\t// Useful when you need to handle multiple acceptable status codes programmatically.\n152\t//\n153\t// Parameters:\n154\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n155\t//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n156\t//\n157\t// Returns:\n158\t//   - true if the response status code is in the allowed list, false otherwise\n159\t//\n160\t// Example:\n161\t//   allowedCodes := []int{200, 201, 204}\n162\t//   if CheckStatusCodeAny(w, allowedCodes) {\n163\t//       // Handle success case\n164\t//   } else {\n165\t//       // Handle error case\n166\t//   }\n167\tfunc CheckStatusCodeAny(response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) bool {\n168\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n169\t\n170\t\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n171\t\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n172\t\t\t\treturn true\n173\t\t\t}\n174\t\t}\n175\t\treturn false\n176\t}\n177\t\n178\t// CheckStatusCodeRange checks if the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n179\t//\n180\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for range-based status code validation.\n181\t// Useful for category-based status code checking (e.g., 2xx, 4xx, 5xx).\n182\t//\n183\t// Parameters:\n184\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n185\t//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n186\t//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n187\t//\n188\t// Returns:\n189\t//   - true if the status code is within the range, false otherwise\n190\t//\n191\t// Example:\n192\t//   // Check for any success code (2xx)\n193\t//   if CheckStatusCodeRange(w, 200, 299) {\n194\t//       // Handle success\n195\t//   }\n196\tfunc CheckStatusCodeRange(response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) bool {\n197\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n198\t\treturn actualCode >= minCode && actualCode <= maxCode\n199\t}\n200\t\n201\t// GetStatusCodeDescription returns a human-readable description for an HTTP status code.\n202\t//\n203\t// This helper provides descriptive names for common HTTP status codes, making\n204\t// test error messages more informative and easier to understand.\n205\t//\n206\t// Parameters:\n207\t//   - code: The HTTP status code\n208\t//\n209\t// Returns:\n210\t//   - A human-readable description string (e.g., \"Not Found\" for 404)\n211\t//\n212\t// Example:\n213\t//   desc := GetStatusCodeDescription(404)\n214\t//   // desc = \"Not Found\"\n215\tfunc GetStatusCodeDescription(code int) string {\n216\t\tdescriptions := map[int]string{\n217\t\t\t100: \"Continue\",\n218\t\t\t101: \"Switching Protocols\",\n219\t\t\t102: \"Processing\",\n220\t\t\t200: \"OK\",\n221\t\t\t201: \"Created\",\n222\t\t\t202: \"Accepted\",\n223\t\t\t203: \"Non-Authoritative Information\",\n224\t\t\t204: \"No Content\",\n225\t\t\t205: \"Reset Content\",\n226\t\t\t206: \"Partial Content\",\n227\t\t\t300: \"Multiple Choices\",\n228\t\t\t301: \"Moved Permanently\",\n229\t\t\t302: \"Found\",\n230\t\t\t303: \"See Other\",\n231\t\t\t304: \"Not Modified\",\n232\t\t\t305: \"Use Proxy\",\n233\t\t\t307: \"Temporary Redirect\",\n234\t\t\t308: \"Permanent Redirect\",\n235\t\t\t400: \"Bad Request\",\n236\t\t\t401: \"Unauthorized\",\n237\t\t\t402: \"Payment Required\",\n238\t\t\t403: \"Forbidden\",\n239\t\t\t404: \"Not Found\",\n240\t\t\t405: \"Method Not Allowed\",\n241\t\t\t406: \"Not Acceptable\",\n242\t\t\t407: \"Proxy Authentication Required\",\n243\t\t\t408: \"Request Timeout\",\n244\t\t\t409: \"Conflict\",\n245\t\t\t410: \"Gone\",\n246\t\t\t411: \"Length Required\",\n247\t\t\t412: \"Precondition Failed\",\n248\t\t\t413: \"Payload Too Large\",\n249\t\t\t414: \"URI Too Long\",\n250\t\t\t415: \"Unsupported Media Type\",\n251\t\t\t416: \"Range Not Satisfiable\",\n252\t\t\t417: \"Expectation Failed\",\n253\t\t\t418: \"I'm a teapot\",\n254\t\t\t422: \"Unprocessable Entity\",\n255\t\t\t423: \"Locked\",\n256\t\t\t424: \"Failed Dependency\",\n257\t\t\t426: \"Upgrade Required\",\n258\t\t\t428: \"Precondition Required\",\n259\t\t\t429: \"Too Many Requests\",\n260\t\t\t431: \"Request Header Fields Too Large\",\n261\t\t\t451: \"Unavailable For Legal Reasons\",\n262\t\t\t500: \"Internal Server Error\",\n263\t\t\t501: \"Not Implemented\",\n264\t\t\t502: \"Bad Gateway\",\n265\t\t\t503: \"Service Unavailable\",\n266\t\t\t504: \"Gateway Timeout\",\n267\t\t\t505: \"HTTP Version Not Supported\",\n268\t\t\t506: \"Variant Also Negotiates\",\n269\t\t\t507: \"Insufficient Storage\",\n270\t\t\t508: \"Loop Detected\",\n271\t\t\t510: \"Not Extended\",\n272\t\t\t511: \"Network Authentication Required\",\n273\t\t}\n274\t\n275\t\tif desc, ok := descriptions[code]; ok {\n276\t\t\treturn desc\n277\t\t}\n278\t\treturn \"Unknown\"\n279\t}\n280\t\n281\t// getRangeDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code range.\n282\t//\n283\t// This helper provides category descriptions for common status code ranges,\n284\t// such as \"Success\" for 2xx codes or \"Client Error\" for 4xx codes.\n285\tfunc getRangeDescription(minCode, maxCode int) string {\n286\t\tranges := map[string]string{\n287\t\t\t\"200-299\": \"Success\",\n288\t\t\t\"300-399\": \"Redirection\",\n289\t\t\t\"400-499\": \"Client Error\",\n290\t\t\t\"500-599\": \"Server Error\",\n291\t\t}\n292\t\n293\t\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n294\t\tif desc, ok := ranges[key]; ok {\n295\t\t\treturn desc\n296\t\t}\n297\t\n298\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Codes %d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n299\t}\n300\t\n301\t// getStatusCode extracts the HTTP status code from various response types.\n302\t//\n303\t// This helper supports both *httptest.ResponseRecorder and *http.Response,\n304\t// making the validation functions work with different response types.\n305\tfunc getStatusCode(response interface{}) int {\n306\t\tswitch r := response.(type) {\n307\t\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n308\t\t\treturn r.Code\n309\t\tcase *http.Response:\n310\t\t\treturn r.StatusCode\n311\t\tdefault:\n312\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"Unsupported response type: %T. Expected *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response\", response))\n313\t\t}\n314\t}\n315\t\n316\t// =============================================================================\n317\t// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON STATUS CODE CATEGORIES\n318\t// =============================================================================\n319\t\n320\t// ValidateSuccessStatusCode validates that the response has a 2xx success status code.\n321\t//\n322\t// This convenience function validates any successful response (200-299).\n323\t// Common success codes include:\n324\t// - 200: OK\n325\t// - 201: Created\n326\t// - 204: No Content\n327\t//\n328\t// Example:\n329\t//   ValidateSuccessStatusCode(t, w)\n330\tfunc ValidateSuccessStatusCode(t *testing.T, response interface{}) {\n331\t\tt.Helper()\n332\t\tValidateStatusCodeRange(t, response, 200, 299)\n333\t}\n334\t\n335\t// ValidateClientErrorStatusCode validates that the response has a 4xx client error status code.\n336\t//\n337\t// This convenience function validates any client error response (400-499).\n338\t// Common client error codes include:\n339\t// - 400: Bad Request\n340\t// - 403: Forbidden\n341\t// - 404: Not Found\n342\t//\n343\t// Example:\n344\t//   ValidateClientErrorStatusCode(t, w)\n345\tfunc ValidateClientErrorStatusCode(t *testing.T, response interface{}) {\n346\t\tt.Helper()\n347\t\tValidateStatusCodeRange(t, response, 400, 499)\n348\t}\n349\t\n350\t// ValidateServerErrorStatusCode validates that the response has a 5xx server error status code.\n351\t//\n352\t// This convenience function validates any server error response (500-599).\n353\t// Common server error codes include:\n354\t// - 500: Internal Server Error\n355\t// - 502: Bad Gateway\n356\t// - 503: Service Unavailable\n357\t//\n358\t// Example:\n359\t//   ValidateServerErrorStatusCode(t, w)\n360\tfunc ValidateServerErrorStatusCode(t *testing.T, response interface{}) {\n361\t\tt.Helper()\n362\t\tValidateStatusCodeRange(t, response, 500, 599)\n363\t}\n364\t\n365\t// ValidateRedirectStatusCode validates that the response has a 3xx redirect status code.\n366\t//\n367\t// This convenience function validates any redirect response (300-399).\n368\t// Common redirect codes include:\n369\t// - server\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"testing\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HTTP STATUS CODE VALIDATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// This module provides comprehensive HTTP status code validation utilities\n// for error response testing. It supports single status codes, multiple allowed\n// status codes, and flexible validation patterns.\n//\n// Functions:\n// - ValidateStatusCode: Validate single expected status code\n// - ValidateStatusCodeAny: Validate against multiple allowed status codes\n// - ValidateStatusCodeRange: Validate status code is within range\n// - CheckStatusCode: Non-asserting version that returns boolean\n// - GetStatusCodeDescription: Get human-readable status code description\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidateStatusCode validates that the HTTP response has the expected status code.\n//\n// This is the basic status code validation helper that checks if a response\n// matches a single expected status code. It provides clear error messages\n// indicating what was expected vs. what was received.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)\nfunc ValidateStatusCode(t *testing.T, response interface{}, expectedCode int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\tif actualCode != expectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code %d (%s), got %d (%s)\",\n\t\t\texpectedCode, GetStatusCodeDescription(expectedCode),\n\t\t\tactualCode, GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode))\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCodeAny validates that the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n//\n// This helper is useful when multiple status codes are acceptable for a given scenario.\n// For example, when testing authentication, you might accept both 403 (Forbidden)\n// and 401 (Unauthorized) depending on the authentication method.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{200, 201, 204})\nfunc ValidateStatusCodeAny(t *testing.T, response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"ValidateStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\n\t// Check if actual code is in the allowed list\n\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n\t\t\treturn // Success - code is allowed\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build helpful error message showing what was allowed vs. what was received\n\tallowedDesc := make([]string, len(allowedCodes))\n\tfor i, code := range allowedCodes {\n\t\tallowedDesc[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, GetStatusCodeDescription(code))\n\t}\n\n\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code to be one of [%v], got %d (%s)\",\n\t\tallowedDesc, actualCode, GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode))\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCodeRange validates that the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n//\n// This helper is useful for validating status codes by category, such as:\n// - 2xx for success responses (200-299)\n// - 4xx for client errors (400-499)\n// - 5xx for server errors (500-599)\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n//\n// Example:\n//   // Validate 2xx success response\n//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 200, 299)\n//\n//   // Validate 4xx client error\n//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 400, 499)\nfunc ValidateStatusCodeRange(t *testing.T, response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif minCode > maxCode {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"ValidateStatusCodeRange: minCode (%d) cannot be greater than maxCode (%d)\", minCode, maxCode)\n\t}\n\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\n\tif actualCode < minCode || actualCode > maxCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code in range [%d-%d] (%s), got %d (%s)\",\n\t\t\tminCode, maxCode, getRangeDescription(minCode, maxCode),\n\t\t\tactualCode, GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode))\n\t}\n}\n\n// CheckStatusCode checks if the HTTP response has the expected status code without asserting.\n//\n// This non-asserting version returns a boolean, allowing for conditional logic\n// in tests. Useful when you need to handle different status codes programmatically\n// rather than failing the test immediately.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if the response has the expected status code, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//   if CheckStatusCode(w, 404) {\n//       // Handle 404 case\n//   } else {\n//       // Handle other cases\n//   }\nfunc CheckStatusCode(response interface{}, expectedCode int) bool {\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\treturn actualCode == expectedCode\n}\n\n// CheckStatusCodeAny checks if the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n//\n// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for flexible status code checking.\n// Useful when you need to handle multiple acceptable status codes programmatically.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if the response status code is in the allowed list, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//   allowedCodes := []int{200, 201, 204}\n//   if CheckStatusCodeAny(w, allowedCodes) {\n//       // Handle success case\n//   } else {\n//       // Handle error case\n//   }\nfunc CheckStatusCodeAny(response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) bool {\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\n\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// CheckStatusCodeRange checks if the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n//\n// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for range-based status code validation.\n// Useful for category-based status code checking (e.g., 2xx, 4xx, 5xx).\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if the status code is within the range, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//   // Check for any success code (2xx)\n//   if CheckStatusCodeRange(w, 200, 299) {\n//       // Handle success\n//   }\nfunc CheckStatusCodeRange(response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) bool {\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\treturn actualCode >= minCode && actualCode <= maxCode\n}\n\n// GetStatusCodeDescription returns a human-readable description for an HTTP status code.\n//\n// This helper provides descriptive names for common HTTP status codes, making\n// test error messages more informative and easier to understand.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - code: The HTTP status code\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - A human-readable description string (e.g., \"Not Found\" for 404)\n//\n// Example:\n//   desc := GetStatusCodeDescription(404)\n//   // desc = \"Not Found\"\nfunc GetStatusCodeDescription(code int) string {\n\tdescriptions := map[int]string{\n\t\t100: \"Continue\",\n\t\t101: \"Switching Protocols\",\n\t\t102: \"Processing\",\n\t\t200: \"OK\",\n\t\t201: \"Created\",\n\t\t202: \"Accepted\",\n\t\t203: \"Non-Authoritative Information\",\n\t\t204: \"No Content\",\n\t\t205: \"Reset Content\",\n\t\t206: \"Partial Content\",\n\t\t300: \"Multiple Storage\",\n\t\t508: \"Loop Detected\",\n\t\t510: \"Not Extended\",\n\t\t511: \"Network Authentication Required\",\n\t}\n\n\tif desc, ok := descriptions[code]; ok {\n\t\treturn desc\n\t}\n\treturn \"Redirection\",\n\t\t\"400-499\": \"Client Error\",\n\t\t\"500-599\": \"Server Error\",\n\t}\n\n\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n\tif desc, ok := ranges[key]; ok {\n\t\treturn desc\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Codes %d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n}\n\n// getStatusCode extracts the HTTP status code from various response types.\n//\n// This helper supports both *httptest.ResponseRecorder and *http.Response,\n// making the validation functions work with different response types.\nfunc getStatusCode(response interface{}) int {\n\tswitch r := response.(type) {\n\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n\t\treturn r.Code\n\tcase *http.Response:\n\t\treturn r.StatusCode\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"Unsupported response type: %T. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_f456049c79074bf58a113a04","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# HTTP Status Code Validation Helper Functions\n2\t\n3\tThis document describes the HTTP status code validation helper functions available in the ARMOR test infrastructure. These helpers provide comprehensive and flexible status code validation for error response testing.\n4\t\n5\t## Overview\n6\t\n7\tThe status code validation helpers are located in `internal/server/error_status_validation.go` and provide:\n8\t\n9\t- **Single status code validation** - Check for exact status code matches\n10\t- **Multiple allowed status codes** - Validate against a list of acceptable codes\n11\t- **Range-based validation** - Check if status code falls within a range (e.g., 2xx, 4xx)\n12\t- **Non-asserting boolean functions** - For conditional logic without test failures\n13\t- **Convenience functions** - For common HTTP status code categories\n14\t- **S3-specific validators** - For common S3 error scenarios\n15\t\n16\t## Function Reference\n17\t\n18\t### Core Validation Functions\n19\t\n20\t#### `ValidateStatusCode(t, response, expectedCode)`\n21\t\n22\tValidates that a response has exactly the expected HTTP status code.\n23\t\n24\t**Parameters:**\n25\t- `t *testing.T` - The testing instance\n26\t- `response` - Response object (*httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n27\t- `expectedCode int` - The expected HTTP status code\n28\t\n29\t**Example:**\n30\t```go\n31\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n32\tw.WriteHeader(404)\n33\t\n34\tValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404) // Passes\n35\tValidateStatusCode(t, w, 200) // Fails with error message\n36\t```\n37\t\n38\t#### `ValidateStatusCodeAny(t, response, allowedCodes)`\n39\t\n40\tValidates that a response has one of the allowed HTTP status codes.\n41\t\n42\t**Parameters:**\n43\t- `t *testing.T` - The testing instance\n44\t- `response` - Response object\n45\t- `allowedCodes []int` - Slice of acceptable status codes\n46\t\n47\t**Example:**\n48\t```go\n49\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n50\tw.WriteHeader(204)\n51\t\n52\tValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{200, 201, 204}) // Passes\n53\tValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{403, 404})     // Fails\n54\t```\n55\t\n56\t#### `ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, response, minCode, maxCode)`\n57\t\n58\tValidates that a response's status code falls within a range.\n59\t\n60\t**Parameters:**\n61\t- `t *testing.T` - The testing instance\n62\t- `response` - Response object\n63\t- `minCode int` - Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n64\t- `maxCode int` - Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n65\t\n66\t**Example:**\n67\t```go\n68\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n69\tw.WriteHeader(204)\n70\t\n71\tValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 200, 299) // Passes (2xx success)\n72\tValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 400, 499) // Fails (not 4xx)\n73\t```\n74\t\n75\t### Non-Asserting Boolean Functions\n76\t\n77\t#### `CheckStatusCode(response, expectedCode) bool`\n78\t\n79\tChecks if a response has the expected status code without asserting.\n80\t\n81\t**Returns:** `true` if status code matches, `false` otherwise\n82\t\n83\t**Example:**\n84\t```go\n85\tif CheckStatusCode(w, 404) {\n86\t    // Handle 404 case\n87\t} else if CheckStatusCode(w, 403) {\n88\t    // Handle 403 case\n89\t}\n90\t```\n91\t\n92\t#### `CheckStatusCodeAny(response, allowedCodes) bool`\n93\t\n94\tChecks if a response has one of the allowed status codes without asserting.\n95\t\n96\t**Returns:** `true` if status code is in allowed list, `false` otherwise\n97\t\n98\t**Example:**\n99\t```go\n100\tallowedCodes := []int{200, 201, 204}\n101\tif CheckStatusCodeAny(w, allowedCodes) {\n102\t    // Handle success case\n103\t}\n104\t```\n105\t\n106\t#### `CheckStatusCodeRange(response, minCode, maxCode) bool`\n107\t\n108\tChecks if a response's status code falls within a range without asserting.\n109\t\n110\t**Returns:** `true` if status code is in range, `false` otherwise\n111\t\n112\t**Example:**\n113\t```go\n114\tif CheckStatusCodeRange(w, 200, 299) {\n115\t    // Handle any success code (2xx)\n116\t}\n117\t```\n118\t\n119\t### Convenience Functions\n120\t\n121\t#### `ValidateSuccessStatusCode(t, response)`\n122\t\n123\tValidates that the response has a 2xx success status code (200-299).\n124\t\n125\t**Common success codes:** 200 OK, 201 Created, 204 No Content\n126\t\n127\t**Example:**\n128\t```go\n129\tValidateSuccessStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 200-299 range\n130\t```\n131\t\n132\t#### `ValidateClientErrorStatusCode(t, response)`\n133\t\n134\tValidates that the response has a 4xx client error status code (400-499).\n135\t\n136\t**Common client error codes:** 400 Bad Request, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found\n137\t\n138\t**Example:**\n139\t```go\n140\tValidateClientErrorStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 400-499 range\n141\t```\n142\t\n143\t#### `ValidateServerErrorStatusCode(t, response)`\n144\t\n145\tValidates that the response has a 5xx server error status code (500-599).\n146\t\n147\t**Common server error codes:** 500 Internal Server Error, 502 Bad Gateway, 503 Service Unavailable\n148\t\n149\t**Example:**\n150\t```go\n151\tValidateServerErrorStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 500-599 range\n152\t```\n153\t\n154\t#### `ValidateRedirectStatusCode(t, response)`\n155\t\n156\tValidates that the response has a 3xx redirect status code (300-399).\n157\t\n158\t**Common redirect codes:** 301 Moved Permanently, 302 Found, 304 Not Modified\n159\t\n160\t**Example:**\n161\t```go\n162\tValidateRedirectStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 300-399 range\n163\t```\n164\t\n165\t### S3-Specific Validators\n166\t\n167\t#### `ValidateS3NotFoundStatusCode(t, response)`\n168\t\n169\tValidates that the response has HTTP 404 (Not Found) - used for missing S3 objects/buckets.\n170\t\n171\t**Example:**\n172\t```go\n173\tValidateS3NotFoundStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 404\n174\t```\n175\t\n176\t#### `ValidateS3AccessDeniedStatusCode(t, response)`\n177\t\n178\tValidates that the response has HTTP 403 (Forbidden) - used for S3 authentication/authorization errors.\n179\t\n180\t**Example:**\n181\t```go\n182\tValidateS3AccessDeniedStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 403\n183\t```\n184\t\n185\t#### `ValidateS3BadRequestStatusCode(t, response)`\n186\t\n187\tValidates that the response has HTTP 400 (Bad Request) - used for S3 validation errors.\n188\t\n189\t**Example:**\n190\t```go\n191\tValidateS3BadRequestStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 400\n192\t```\n193\t\n194\t### Utility Functions\n195\t\n196\t#### `GetStatusCodeDescription(code int) string`\n197\t\n198\tReturns a human-readable description for an HTTP status code.\n199\t\n200\t**Example:**\n201\t```go\n202\tdesc := GetStatusCodeDescription(404)\n203\t// desc = \"Not Found\"\n204\t\n205\tdesc = GetStatusCodeDescription(500)\n206\t// desc = \"Internal Server Error\"\n207\t```\n208\t\n209\t**Supported codes include:**\n210\t- 1xx: Informational (100 Continue, 101 Switching Protocols, 102 Processing)\n211\t- 2xx: Success (200 OK, 201 Created, 202 Accepted, 204 No Content, 206 Partial Content)\n212\t- 3xx: Redirection (300 Multiple Choices, 301 Moved Permanently, 302 Found, 304 Not Modified, 307/308 Redirects)\n213\t- 4xx: Client Errors (400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found, 405 Method Not Allowed, 409 Conflict, 410 Gone, 422 Unprocessable Entity, 429 Too Many Requests)\n214\t- 5xx: Server Errors (500 Internal Server Error, 502 Bad Gateway, 503 Service Unavailable, 504 Gateway Timeout)\n215\t\n216\t## Usage Patterns\n217\t\n218\t### Basic Error Response Validation\n219\t\n220\t```go\n221\tfunc TestMyError(t *testing.T) {\n222\t    fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n223\t    \n224\t    req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/nonexistent\", nil, nil)\n225\t    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n226\t    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n227\t    \n228\t    // Validate status code\n229\t    ValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)\n230\t    \n231\t    // Or use S3-specific validator\n232\t    ValidateS3NotFoundStatusCode(t, w)\n233\t}\n234\t```\n235\t\n236\t### Multiple Acceptable Status Codes\n237\t\n238\t```go\n239\tfunc TestFlexibleResponse(t *testing.T) {\n240\t    fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n241\t    \n242\t    req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"PUT\", \"/test-bucket/key\", nil, nil)\n243\t    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n244\t    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n245\t    \n246\t    // Accept either 200 OK or 201 Created\n247\t    ValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{200, 201})\n248\t}\n249\t```\n250\t\n251\t### Category-Based Validation\n252\t\n253\t```go\n254\tfunc TestErrorCategories(t *testing.T) {\n255\t    fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n256\t    \n257\t    // Test client error (4xx)\n258\t    req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"POST\", \"/test-bucket/\", nil, nil)\n259\t    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n260\t    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n261\t    ValidateClientErrorStatusCode(t, w) // 400-499\n262\t    \n263\t    // Test success (2xx)\n264\t    req2 := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key\", nil, nil)\n265\t    w2 := httptest.NewRecorder()\n266\t    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w2, req2)\n267\t    ValidateSuccessStatusCode(t, w2) // 200-299\n268\t}\n269\t```\n270\t\n271\t### Conditional Logic Without Assertions\n272\t\n273\t```go\n274\tfunc TestConditionalHandling(t *testing.T) {\n275\t    fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n276\t    \n277\t    req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key\", nil, nil)\n278\t    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n279\t    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n280\t    \n281\t    if CheckStatusCode(w, 404) {\n282\t        t.Log(\"Key not found - expected behavior\")\n283\t    } else if CheckStatusCodeAny(w, []int{403, 401}) {\n284\t        t.Log(\"Access denied - check credentials\")\n285\t    } else if CheckStatusCodeRange(w, 200, 299) {\n286\t        t.Log(\"Success - key found\")\n287\t    } else {\n288\t        t.Errorf(\"Unexpected status code: %d\", w.Code)\n289\t    }\n290\t}\n291\t```\n292\t\n293\t### Integration with Error Response Validator\n294\t\n295\t```go\n296\tfunc TestCompleteValidation(t *testing.T) {\n297\t    fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n298\t    \n299\t    req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/nonexistent\", nil, nil)\n300\t    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n301\t    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n302\t    \n303\t    // Combine status code validation with other validations\n304\t    VerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n305\t        HTTPStatusCode(404).\n306\t        ContentType(\"application/xml\").\n307\t        HasCode(\"NoSuchKey\").\n308\t        MessageMinLength(15).\n309\t        Assert()\n310\t}\n311\t```\n312\t\n313\t## Error Messages\n314\t\n315\tThe validation functions provide clear, informative error messages that include:\n316\t\n317\t1. **Expected vs. actual status codes** - Shows what was expected and what was received\n318\t2. **Human-readable descriptions** - Includes status code names (e.g., \"Not Found\" for 404)\n319\t3. **Allowed code lists** - For multiple code validation, shows all acceptable codes\n320\t4. **Range descriptions** - For range validation, shows the category (e.g., \"Success\", \"Client Error\")\n321\t\n322\t**Example error messages:**\n323\t\n324\t```\n325\tExpected HTTP status code 404 (Not Found), got 200 (OK)\n326\tExpected HTTP status code to be one of [[200 (OK) 201 (Created) 204 (No Content)]], got 404 (Not Found)\n327\tExpected HTTP status code in range [200-299] (Success), got 400 (Bad Request)\n328\t```\n329\t\n330\t## Testing\n331\t\n332\tThe validation helpers are thoroughly tested in `internal/server/error_status_validation_test.go`. Test coverage includes:\n333\t\n334\t- Single status code validation (positive and negative cases)\n335\t- Multiple allowed status codes\n336\t- Status code range validation\n337\t- Non-asserting boolean functions\n338\t- Status code descriptions\n339\t- Convenience functions for all categories\n340\t- S3-specific validators\n341\t- Integration tests with real HTTP responses\n342\t- Real-world usage scenarios\n343\t\n344\tRun the tests with:\n345\t```bash\n346\tgo test -v ./internal/server -run TestValidateStatusCode\n347\tgo test -v ./internal/server -run TestCheckStatusCode\n348\tgo test -v ./internal/server -run TestValidateSuccessStatusCode\n349\tgo test -v ./internal/server -run TestValidateS3\n350\t```\n351\t\n352\t## Best Practices\n353\t\n354\t1. **Use specific validators when possible** - Instead of `ValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)`, use `ValidateS3NotFoundStatusCode(t, w)` for S3 tests\n355\t2. **Combine with other validations** - Use status code validation alongside content-type, error code, and message validation\n356\t3. **Use range validation for categories** - Use `ValidateClientErrorStatusCode(t, w)` instead of checking multiple specific codes\n357\t4. **Leverage boolean functions for complex logic** - Use `CheckStatusCode*` functions when you need conditional behavior\n358\t5. **Test both success and failure paths** - Ensure your tests cover both expected and unexpected status codes\n359\t\n360\t## Migration Guide\n361\t\n362\tIf you're currently using manual status code checking:\n363\t\n364\t**Before:**\n365\t```go\n366\tif w.Code != 404 {\n367\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected 404, got %d\", w.Code)\n368\t}\n369\t```\n370\t\n371\t**After:**\n372\t```go\n373\tValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)\n374\t// Or for S3 errors:\n375\tValidateS3NotFoundStatusCode(t, w)\n376\t```\n377\t\n378\t**Before:**\n379\t```go\n380\tif w.Code < 200 || w.Code > 299 {\n381\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected success code, got %d\", w.Code)\n382\t}\n383\t```\n384\t\n385\t**After:**\n386\t```go\n387\tValidateSuccessStatusCode(t, w)\n388\t```"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"313d8101-43ff-4d13-9d92-77ad59d5223a","uuid":"0a2aa816-7f2d-4d59-96fc-a0c75b5ecd24","timestamp":"2026-07-14T07:19:36.622Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/docs/http-status-code-validation-helpers.md","content":"# HTTP Status Code Validation Helper Functions\n\nThis document describes the HTTP status code validation helper functions available in the ARMOR test infrastructure. These helpers provide comprehensive and flexible status code validation for error response testing.\n\n## Overview\n\nThe status code validation helpers are located in `internal/server/error_status_validation.go` and provide:\n\n- **Single status code validation** - Check for exact status code matches\n- **Multiple allowed status codes** - Validate against a list of acceptable codes\n- **Range-based validation** - Check if status code falls within a range (e.g., 2xx, 4xx)\n- **Non-asserting boolean functions** - For conditional logic without test failures\n- **Convenience functions** - For common HTTP status code categories\n- **S3-specific validators** - For common S3 error scenarios\n\n## Function Reference\n\n### Core Validation Functions\n\n#### `ValidateStatusCode(t, response, expectedCode)`\n\nValidates that a response has exactly the expected HTTP status code.\n\n**Parameters:**\n- `t *testing.T` - The testing instance\n- `response` - Response object (*httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n- `expectedCode int` - The expected HTTP status code\n\n**Example:**\n```go\nw := httptest.NewRecorder()\nw.WriteHeader(404)\n\nValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404) // Passes\nValidateStatusCode(t, w, 200) // Fails with error message\n```\n\n#### `ValidateStatusCodeAny(t, response, allowedCodes)`\n\nValidates that a response has one of the allowed HTTP status codes.\n\n**Parameters:**\n- `t *testing.T` - The testing instance\n- `response` - Response object\n- `allowedCodes []int` - Slice of acceptable status codes\n\n**Example:**\n```go\nw := httptest.NewRecorder()\nw.WriteHeader(204)\n\nValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{200, 201, 204}) // Passes\nValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{403, 404})     // Fails\n```\n\n#### `ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, response, minCode, maxCode)`\n\nValidates that a response's status code falls within a range.\n\n**Parameters:**\n- `t *testing.T` - The testing instance\n- `response` - Response object\n- `minCode int` - Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n- `maxCode int` - Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n\n**Example:**\n```go\nw := httptest.NewRecorder()\nw.WriteHeader(204)\n\nValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 200, 299) // Passes (2xx success)\nValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 400, 499) // Fails (not 4xx)\n```\n\n### Non-Asserting Boolean Functions\n\n#### `CheckStatusCode(response, expectedCode) bool`\n\nChecks if a response has the expected status code without asserting.\n\n**Returns:** `true` if status code matches, `false` otherwise\n\n**Example:**\n```go\nif CheckStatusCode(w, 404) {\n    // Handle 404 case\n} else if CheckStatusCode(w, 403) {\n    // Handle 403 case\n}\n```\n\n#### `CheckStatusCodeAny(response, allowedCodes) bool`\n\nChecks if a response has one of the allowed status codes without asserting.\n\n**Returns:** `true` if status code is in allowed list, `false` otherwise\n\n**Example:**\n```go\nallowedCodes := []int{200, 201, 204}\nif CheckStatusCodeAny(w, allowedCodes) {\n    // Handle success case\n}\n```\n\n#### `CheckStatusCodeRange(response, minCode, maxCode) bool`\n\nChecks if a response's status code falls within a range without asserting.\n\n**Returns:** `true` if status code is in range, `false` otherwise\n\n**Example:**\n```go\nif CheckStatusCodeRange(w, 200, 299) {\n    // Handle any success code (2xx)\n}\n```\n\n### Convenience Functions\n\n#### `ValidateSuccessStatusCode(t, response)`\n\nValidates that the response has a 2xx success status code (200-299).\n\n**Common success codes:** 200 OK, 201 Created, 204 No Content\n\n**Example:**\n```go\nValidateSuccessStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 200-299 range\n```\n\n#### `ValidateClientErrorStatusCode(t, response)`\n\nValidates that the response has a 4xx client error status code (400-499).\n\n**Common client error codes:** 400 Bad Request, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found\n\n**Example:**\n```go\nValidateClientErrorStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 400-499 range\n```\n\n#### `ValidateServerErrorStatusCode(t, response)`\n\nValidates that the response has a 5xx server error status code (500-599).\n\n**Common server error codes:** 500 Internal Server Error, 502 Bad Gateway, 503 Service Unavailable\n\n**Example:**\n```go\nValidateServerErrorStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 500-599 range\n```\n\n#### `ValidateRedirectStatusCode(t, response)`\n\nValidates that the response has a 3xx redirect status code (300-399).\n\n**Common redirect codes:** 301 Moved Permanently, 302 Found, 304 Not Modified\n\n**Example:**\n```go\nValidateRedirectStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 300-399 range\n```\n\n### S3-Specific Validators\n\n#### `ValidateS3NotFoundStatusCode(t, response)`\n\nValidates that the response has HTTP 404 (Not Found) - used for missing S3 objects/buckets.\n\n**Example:**\n```go\nValidateS3NotFoundStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 404\n```\n\n#### `ValidateS3AccessDeniedStatusCode(t, response)`\n\nValidates that the response has HTTP 403 (Forbidden) - used for S3 authentication/authorization errors.\n\n**Example:**\n```go\nValidateS3AccessDeniedStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 403\n```\n\n#### `ValidateS3BadRequestStatusCode(t, response)`\n\nValidates that the response has HTTP 400 (Bad Request) - used for S3 validation errors.\n\n**Example:**\n```go\nValidateS3BadRequestStatusCode(t, w) // Validates 400\n```\n\n### Utility Functions\n\n#### `GetStatusCodeDescription(code int) string`\n\nReturns a human-readable description for an HTTP status code.\n\n**Example:**\n```go\ndesc := GetStatusCodeDescription(404)\n// desc = \"Not Found\"\n\ndesc = GetStatusCodeDescription(500)\n// desc = \"Internal Server Error\"\n```\n\n**Supported codes include:**\n- 1xx: Informational (100 Continue, 101 Switching Protocols, 102 Processing)\n- 2xx: Success (200 OK, 201 Created, 202 Accepted, 204 No Content, 206 Partial Content)\n- 3xx: Redirection (300 Multiple Choices, 301 Moved Permanently, 302 Found, 304 Not Modified, 307/308 Redirects)\n- 4xx: Client Errors (400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found, 405 Method Not Allowed, 409 Conflict, 410 Gone, 422 Unprocessable Entity, 429 Too Many Requests)\n- 5xx: Server Errors (500 Internal Server Error, 502 Bad Gateway, 503 Service Unavailable, 504 Gateway Timeout)\n\n## Usage Patterns\n\n### Basic Error Response Validation\n\n```go\nfunc TestMyError(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n    \n    req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/nonexistent\", nil, nil)\n    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    \n    // Validate status code\n    ValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)\n    \n    // Or use S3-specific validator\n    ValidateS3NotFoundStatusCode(t, w)\n}\n```\n\n### Multiple Acceptable Status Codes\n\n```go\nfunc TestFlexibleResponse(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n    \n    req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"PUT\", \"/test-bucket/key\", nil, nil)\n    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    \n    // Accept either 200 OK or 201 Created\n    ValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{200, 201})\n}\n```\n\n### Category-Based Validation\n\n```go\nfunc TestErrorCategories(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n    \n    // Test client error (4xx)\n    req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"POST\", \"/test-bucket/\", nil, nil)\n    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    ValidateClientErrorStatusCode(t, w) // 400-499\n    \n    // Test success (2xx)\n    req2 := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key\", nil, nil)\n    w2 := httptest.NewRecorder()\n    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w2, req2)\n    ValidateSuccessStatusCode(t, w2) // 200-299\n}\n```\n\n### Conditional Logic Without Assertions\n\n```go\nfunc TestConditionalHandling(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n    \n    req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key\", nil, nil)\n    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    \n    if CheckStatusCode(w, 404) {\n        t.Log(\"Key not found - expected behavior\")\n    } else if CheckStatusCodeAny(w, []int{403, 401}) {\n        t.Log(\"Access denied - check credentials\")\n    } else if CheckStatusCodeRange(w, 200, 299) {\n        t.Log(\"Success - key found\")\n    } else {\n        t.Errorf(\"Unexpected status code: %d\", w.Code)\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Integration with Error Response Validator\n\n```go\nfunc TestCompleteValidation(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n    \n    req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/nonexistent\", nil, nil)\n    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    \n    // Combine status code validation with other validations\n    VerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n        HTTPStatusCode(404).\n        ContentType(\"application/xml\").\n        HasCode(\"NoSuchKey\").\n        MessageMinLength(15).\n        Assert()\n}\n```\n\n## Error Messages\n\nThe validation functions provide clear, informative error messages that include:\n\n1. **Expected vs. actual status codes** - Shows what was expected and what was received\n2. **Human-readable descriptions** - Includes status code names (e.g., \"Not Found\" for 404)\n3. **Allowed code lists** - For multiple code validation, shows all acceptable codes\n4. **Range descriptions** - For range validation, shows the category (e.g., \"Success\", \"Client Error\")\n\n**Example error messages:**\n\n```\nExpected HTTP status code 404 (Not Found), got 200 (OK)\nExpected HTTP status code to be one of [[200 (OK) 201 (Created) 204 (No Content)]], got 404 (Not Found)\nExpected HTTP status code in range [200-299] (Success), got 400 (Bad Request)\n```\n\n## Testing\n\nThe validation helpers are thoroughly tested in `internal/server/error_status_validation_test.go`. Test coverage includes:\n\n- Single status code validation (positive and negative cases)\n- Multiple allowed status codes\n- Status code range validation\n- Non-asserting boolean functions\n- Status code descriptions\n- Convenience functions for all categories\n- S3-specific validators\n- Integration tests with real HTTP responses\n- Real-world usage scenarios\n\nRun the tests with:\n```bash\ngo test -v ./internal/server -run TestValidateStatusCode\ngo test -v ./internal/server -run TestCheckStatusCode\ngo test -v ./internal/server -run TestValidateSuccessStatusCode\ngo test -v ./internal/server -run TestValidateS3\n```\n\n## Best Practices\n\n1. **Use specific validators when possible** - Instead of `ValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)`, use `ValidateS3NotFoundStatusCode(t, w)` for S3 tests\n2. **Combine with other validations** - Use status code validation alongside content-type, error code, and message validation\n3. **Use range validation for categories** - Use `ValidateClientErrorStatusCode(t, w)` instead of checking multiple specific codes\n4. **Leverage boolean functions for complex logic** - Use `CheckStatusCode*` functions when you need conditional behavior\n5. **Test both success and failure paths** - Ensure your tests cover both expected and unexpected status codes\n\n## Migration Guide\n\nIf you're currently using manual status code checking:\n\n**Before:**\n```go\nif w.Code != 404 {\n    t.Errorf(\"Expected 404, got %d\", w.Code)\n}\n```\n\n**After:**\n```go\nValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)\n// Or for S3 errors:\nValidateS3NotFoundStatusCode(t, w)\n```\n\n**Before:**\n```go\nif w.Code < 200 || w.Code > 299 {\n    t.Errorf(\"Expected success code, got %d\", w.Code)\n}\n```\n\n**After:**\n```go\nValidateSuccessStatusCode(t, w)\n```","numLines":388,"startLine":1,"totalLines":388}}}

The tests are working correctly - they're designed to test both success and failure cases.\n\nHowever, the way the tests are written is problematic. The negative test cases are not marked as `t.Run` with expected failures - they just call the validation functions and expect them to fail, which causes the test framework to mark them as failures.\n\nLet me look at the test more carefully. In the failure tests, the code calls `ValidateStatusCode` but doesn't actually fail the test if it doesn't error out. The tests are designed to show that when you call `ValidateStatusCode(t, w, expectedCode)` and the codes don't match, it calls `t.Errorf()` which causes the test to fail.\n\nThe issue is that these are expected failures in the test logic - the tests are demonstrating that the validation functions correctly fail when given mismatched status codes. The tests are doing exactly what they should be doing.\n\nHowever, from a testing perspective, these tests are problematic because they will always fail. A better approach would be to:\n1. Use `testify/require` or similar to assert that the function panics or fails\n2. Or restructure the tests to not actually fail the test run\n\nBut wait - looking at the output more carefully, I see that the implementation is actually working correctly. The tests that are \"failing\" are the ones designed to test that the validation functions properly report errors when status codes don't match. \n\nThe problem is that the negative test cases don't prevent the test from failing in the test framework. When `ValidateStatusCode` calls `t.Errorf()`, it marks the test as failed in the test framework's eyes, even though this is the intended behavior for those test cases.\n\nA proper way to handle this would be to either:\n1. Capture and verify that the error was reported (perhaps using a custom test helper)\n2. Mark these as subtests that are expected to fail\n\n\n3. Restructure to use the boolean Check functions instead of the Validate functions\n\nI'll use the boolean check approach to validate the negative scenarios. This method allows me to explicitly test the error conditions without causing the entire test to fail. By using `CheckStatusCode` and `false`, I can verify that the validation logic correctly identifies mismatches without triggering test framework errors.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"313d8101-43ff-4d13-9d92-77ad59d5223a","uuid":"22f268b4-cb62-406b-b3ad-e9f4fc90b23d"}

*ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tif ev.s3Error == nil {\n\t\t\t\tif err := ev.parse(); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\treturn append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"HasXMLDeclaration\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tbody := v.response.Body.String()\n\t\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(body, \"<?xml\") {\n\t\t\t\tv.t.Errorf(\"Expected XML declaration, got: %s\", body[:min(len(body), 50)])\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// BodyNotEmpty verifies the response body is not empty.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) BodyNotEmpty() *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"BodyNotEmpty\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tif v.response.Body.Len() == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tv.t.Error(\"Expected non-empty response body\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// MessageContainsAny verifies the message contains at least one of the keywords.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) MessageContainsAny(keywords ...string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"MessageContainsAny\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tif ev.s3Error == nil {\n\t\t\t\tif err := ev.parse(); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tmessage := strings.ToLower(ev.s3Error.Message)\n\t\t\tfound := false\n\t\t\tfor _, keyword := range keywords {\n\t\t\t\tif strings.Contains(message, strings.ToLower(keyword)) {\n\t\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\t\tv.t.Errorf(\"Error message should contain at least one of %v, got: %s\",\n\t\t\t\t\tkeywords, ev.s3Error.Message)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// HasCORSHeaders verifies that CORS headers are present on the error response.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) HasCORSHeaders() *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"HasCORSHeaders\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\torigin := ev.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\t\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' to be set\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tmethods := ev.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\t\t\tif methods == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' to be set\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\theaders := ev.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\t\t\tif headers == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' to be set\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// CORSOrigin verifies the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin header value.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) CORSOrigin(origin string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"CORSOrigin\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tactual := v.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\t\t\tif actual != origin {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected CORS origin '%s', got '%s'\", origin, actual)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// CORSMethods verifies the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Methods header value.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) CORSMethods(methods string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"CORSMethods\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tactual := v.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\t\t\tif actual != methods {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected CORS methods '%s', got '%s'\", methods, actual)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// CORSHeaders verifies the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Headers header value.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) CORSHeaders(headers string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"CORSHeaders\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tactual := v.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\t\t\tif actual != headers {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected CORS headers '%s', got '%s'\", headers, actual)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// Assert runs all validations and asserts if any fail.\n// This is the terminal method - call it to execute all validations.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) Assert() {\n\tv.t.Helper()\n\n\t// Parse response once for all validations\n\tif err := v.parse(); err != nil {\n\t\tv.t.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Run all validations\n\tfor _, exp := range v.expectations {\n\t\tif err := exp.validate(v); err != nil {\n\t\t\texp.failed = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// parse parses the S3 error response.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) parse() error {\n\tif v.s3Error != nil {\n\t\treturn nil // Already parsed\n\t}\n\n\tif v.response.Body.Len() == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil // Nothing to parse\n\t}\n\n\tv.s3Error = &S3Error{}\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(v.response.Body.Bytes(), v.s3Error); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SIMPLE VALIDATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// VerifyStandardErrorResponse performs standard error response validation.\n// This is a convenience function for common validation patterns.\n//\n// It verifies:\n// - HTTP status code matches expected\n// - Content-Type is \"application/xml\"\n// - Response body is not empty\n// - Error code matches expected\n// - Error message is at least 15 characters\n// - Response starts with XML declaration\nfunc VerifyStandardErrorResponse(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string, expectedStatus int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tVerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n\t\tHTTPStatusCode(expectedStatus).\n\t\tContentType(\"application/xml\").\n\t\tBodyNotEmpty().\n\t\tHasCode(expectedCode).\n\t\tMessageMinLength(15).\n\t\tHasXMLDeclaration().\n\t\tAssert()\n}\n\n// ParseErrorResponse parses an S3 error response from the response body.\nfunc ParseErrorResponse(t *testing.T, body []byte) *S3Error {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn &s3Err\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CORE VALIDATION HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n// These are standalone helper functions for validating HTTP error responses.\n// They provide a simpler alternative to the fluent ErrorResponseValidator API.\n// Each helper performs a single validation and can be used independently.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidateHTTPStatusCode validates the HTTP status code of an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the response has the expected HTTP status code.\n// It's useful for verifying that error responses return the correct status codes\n// (e.g., 404 for NotFound, 403 for Forbidden, 400 for BadRequest).\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateHTTPStatusCode(t, w, 404)\nfunc ValidateHTTPStatusCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif w.Code != expectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code %d, got %d\", expectedCode, w.Code)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateContentType validates the Content-Type header of an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the response has the expected Content-Type header.\n// For S3-compatible error responses, this should typically be \"application/xml\".\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateContentType(t, w, \"application/xml\")\nfunc ValidateContentType(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedContentType string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualContentType := w.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif actualContentType != expectedContentType {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected Content-Type '%s', got '%s'\", expectedContentType, actualContentType)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorStructure validates that an error response has proper structure.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response contains:\n// - A non-empty response body\n// - A valid S3 Error XML structure with Code and Message fields\n// - A non-empty error code\n// - A non-empty error message\n//\n// This ensures the error response is well-formed and contains the required fields.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateErrorStructure(t, w)\nfunc ValidateErrorStructure(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Check response body is not empty\n\tif w.Body.Len() == 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response body is empty\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse the error response\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\t// Validate error code is present\n\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Code field\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate error message is present\n\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Message field\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorCode validates the S3 error code in an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response contains the expected S3 error code.\n// Common S3 error codes include: NoSuchKey, AccessDenied, InvalidRequest, etc.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateErrorCode(t, w, \"NoSuchKey\")\nfunc ValidateErrorCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\tif s3Err.Code != expectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSHeaders validates that CORS headers are present on an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response includes the required CORS headers:\n// - Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n// - Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n// - Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n//\n// This ensures that error responses properly support cross-origin requests.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateCORSHeaders(t, w)\nfunc ValidateCORSHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n\torigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\tif origin == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' to be set\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n\tmethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\tif methods == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' to be set\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n\theaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\tif headers == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' to be set\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSOrigin validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin header value.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS origin.\n// Common values are \"*\" (allow all origins) or a specific origin.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateCORSOrigin(t, w, \"*\")\nfunc ValidateCORSOrigin(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedOrigin string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualOrigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\tif actualOrigin != expectedOrigin {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS origin '%s', got '%s'\", expectedOrigin, actualOrigin)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSMethods validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Methods header value.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS methods.\n// Common values include: \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\"\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateCORSMethods(t, w, \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\")\nfunc ValidateCORSMethods(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedMethods string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualMethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\tif actualMethods != expectedMethods {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS methods '%s', got '%s'\", expectedMethods, actualMethods)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSAllowHeaders validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Headers header value.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS headers.\n// Common values include: \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\"\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t, w, \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\")\nfunc ValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedHeaders string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualHeaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\tif actualHeaders != expectedHeaders {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS allow-headers '%s', got '%s'\", expectedHeaders, actualHeaders)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessageContains validates that the error message contains expected text.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response message contains the specified substring.\n// This is useful for verifying that error messages are descriptive and helpful.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateErrorMessageContains(t, w, \"not found\")\nfunc ValidateErrorMessageContains(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedText string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\tif !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, expectedText) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedText, s3Err.Message)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessageMinLength validates that the error message meets minimum length.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response message is at least the specified\n// number of characters. This ensures error messages are sufficiently descriptive.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t, w, 15)\nfunc ValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, minLength int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\tif len(s3Err.Message) < minLength {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), minLength, s3Err.Message)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateXMLDeclaration validates that the response starts with an XML declaration.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response begins with \"<?xml\" which indicates\n// a proper XML declaration. S3 error responses should be valid XML documents.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateXMLDeclaration(t, w)\nfunc ValidateXMLDeclaration(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tbody := w.Body.String()\n\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(body, \"<?xml\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected response to start with XML declaration, got: %s\", body[:min(len(body), 50)])\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// REQUEST CREATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// CreateTestRequest creates an HTTP request for testing.\n//\n// This helper centralizes request creation and ensures consistent test setup.\n// It supports:\n// - Custom HTTP methods and paths\n// - Optional request body\n// - Custom headers\n// - Test server URL format\nfunc CreateTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string, body io.Reader, headers map[string]string) *http.Request {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treq := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, body)\n\n\t// Set default headers for test requests\n\tif headers == nil {\n\t\theaders = make(map[string]string)\n\t}\n\n\t// Set custom headers\n\tfor key, value := range headers {\n\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n\t}\n\n\treturn req\n}\n\n// CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest creates a properly authenticated test request.\n//\n// This helper creates a request with valid AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 authentication.\n// It's useful for testing non-authentication errors (where auth should succeed).\nfunc CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string) *http.Request {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Use the working authentication helper from auth_integration_test.go\n\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, method, path, \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n}\n\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TEST SCENARIO STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorScenario represents a testable error scenario.\ntype ErrorScenario struct {\n\t// Name is the scenario name for test reporting\n\tName string\n\n\t// SetupRequest creates the request that triggers the error\n\tSetupRequest func(*testing.T) *http.Request\n\n\t// ExpectedCode is the expected S3 error code\n\tExpectedCode string\n\n\t// ExpectedStatus is the expected HTTP status code\n\tExpectedStatus int\n\n\t// ExpectedKeywords are keywords that should appear in the error message\n\tExpectedKeywords []string\n\n\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum acceptable message length\n\tMinMessageLength int\n\n\t// MaxResponseTime is the maximum acceptable response duration\n\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration\n\n\t// Description explains what this scenario tests\n\tDescription string\n}\n\n// ErrorScenarioResult captures the result of running a scenario.\ntype ErrorScenarioResult struct {\n\tScenarioName string\n\tStatusCode   int\n\tErrorCode    string\n\tMessage      string\n\tDuration     time.Duration\n\tPassed       bool\n}\n\n// RunErrorScenario executes a single error scenario and returns the result.\nfunc RunErrorScenario(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorScenarioResult {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treq := scenario.SetupRequest(t)\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\n\t// Measure response time\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\tfixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\tduration := time.Since(start)\n\n\t// Parse response\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\txml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err)\n\n\t// Build result\n\tresult := &ErrorScenarioResult{\n\t\tScenarioName: scenario.Name,\n\t\tStatusCode:   w.Code,\n\t\tErrorCode:    s3Err.Code,\n\t\tMessage:      s3Err.Message,\n\t\tDuration:     duration,\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate result\n\tresult.Passed = true\n\n\tif w.Code != scenario.ExpectedStatus {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected status %d, got %d\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedStatus, w.Code)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\tif s3Err.Code != scenario.ExpectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\tif len(s3Err.Message) < scenario.MinMessageLength {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n\t\t\tscenario.Name, len(s3Err.Message), scenario.MinMessageLength, s3Err.Message)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check keywords if specified\n\tif len(scenario.ExpectedKeywords) > 0 {\n\t\tmessage := strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tfound := false\n\t\tfor _, keyword := range scenario.ExpectedKeywords {\n\t\t\tif strings.Contains(message, strings.ToLower(keyword)) {\n\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Error message should contain at least one of %v, got: %s\",\n\t\t\t\tscenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedKeywords, s3Err.Message)\n\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check response time if specified\n\tif scenario.MaxResponseTime > 0 && duration > scenario.MaxResponseTime {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Response time %v exceeds maximum %v\", scenario.Name, duration, scenario.MaxResponseTime)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// BASE TEST STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorTestSuite provides a base structure for error response test suites.\n//\n// This structure helps organize related error tests and provides\n// consistent reporting and validation across test types.\ntype ErrorTestSuite struct {\n\tt          *testing.T\n\tname       string\n\tfixture    *TestServerFixture\n\tscenarios  []ErrorScenario\n\tresults    []*ErrorScenarioResult\n}\n\n// NewErrorTestSuite creates a new error test suite.\nfunc NewErrorTestSuite(t *testing.T, name string) *ErrorTestSuite {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treturn &ErrorTestSuite{\n\t\tt:       t,\n\t\tname:    name,\n\t\tfixture: NewTestServer(t),\n\t\tresults: make([]*ErrorScenarioResult, 0),\n\t}\n}\n\n// AddScenario adds a test scenario to the suite.\nfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) AddScenario(scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorTestSuite {\n\tsuite.scenarios = append(suite.scenarios, scenario)\n\treturn suite\n}\n\n// Run executes all scenarios in the suite.\nfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) Run() {\n\tsuite.t.Helper()\n\n\tsuite.t.Run(suite.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tfor _, scenario := range suite.scenarios {\n\t\t\tt.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tresult := RunErrorScenario(t, suite.fixture, scenario)\n\t\t\t\tsuite.results = append(suite.results, result)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// RunSequentially executes all scenarios sequentially (not in parallel).\n// Use this when scenarios have dependencies or share state.\nfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) RunSequentially() {\n\tsuite.t.Helper()\n\n\tsuite.t.Run(suite.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tfor _, scenario := range suite.scenarios {\n\t\t\tt.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Parallel() // Disable parallelism\n\t\t\t\tresult := RunErrorScenario(t, suite.fixture, scenario)\n\t\t\t\tsuite.results = append(suite.results, result)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// ReportStatistics logs performance statistics for the suite.\nfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) ReportStatistics() {\n\tsuite.t.Helper()\n\n\tif len(suite.results) == 0 {\n\t\tsuite.t.Log(\"No results to report\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tvar total, max, min time.Duration\n\tmin = 1 * time.Second // Initialize to high value\n\tpassed := 0\n\tfailed := 0\n\n\tfor _, result := range suite.results {\n\t\ttotal += result.Duration\n\t\tif result.Duration > max {\n\t\t\tmax = result.Duration\n\t\t}\n\t\tif result.Duration < min {\n\t\t\tmin = result.Duration\n\t\t}\n\t\tif result.Passed {\n\t\t\tpassed++\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfailed++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tavg := total / time.Duration(len(suite.results))\n\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"Error Test Suite: %s\", suite.name)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Total scenarios: %d\", len(suite.results))\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Passed: %d, Failed: %d\", passed, failed)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Average response time: %v\", avg)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Min response time: %v\", min)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Max response time: %v\", max)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// UTILITY FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// min returns the minimum of two integers.\nfunc min(a, b int) int {\n\tif a < b {\n\t\treturn a\n\t}\n\treturn b\n}\n\n// MeasureRequestTime measures the time to execute a request.\n// Returns the duration and response recorder.\nfunc MeasureRequestTime(handler http.Handler, req *http.Request) (time.Duration, *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\tduration := time.Since(start)\n\treturn duration, w\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// DOCUMENTATION AND EXAMPLES\n// =============================================================================\n\n/*\nExample Usage:\n\nBasic error response validation:\n\n    func TestMyError(t *testing.T) {\n        fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n\n        req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/nonexistent\", nil, nil)\n        w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n        fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n        VerifyStandardErrorResponse(t, w, \"NoSuchKey\", 404)\n    }\n\nAdvanced validation with fluent API:\n\n    func TestMyErrorAdvanced(t *testing.T) {\n        fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n\n        req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"POST\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil, nil)\n        w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n        fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n        duration, _ :=
